moduleSettings.settingsXML:
<gp_project_root ver="1">
  <gallery>
    <tool name="PairwiseBuffer" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Default">
      <displayname>Pairwise Buffer</displayname>
      <description>Creates buffer polygons around input features to a specified distance using a parallel processing approach.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="SummarizeWithin" type="script" toolboxalias="analysis" gallery_group="Default">
      <displayname>Summarize Within</displayname>
      <description>Overlays a polygon layer with another layer to summarize the number of points, length of the lines, or area of the polygons within each polygon, and calculate attribute field statistics about the features within the polygons.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="SpatialJoin" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" gallery_group="Default">
      <displayname>Spatial Join</displayname>
      <description>Joins attributes from one feature to another based on the spatial relationship. The target features and the joined attributes from the join features are written to the output feature class.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="PairwiseClip" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Default">
      <displayname>Pairwise Clip</displayname>
      <description>Extracts input features that overlay the clip features.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="OptimizedHotSpotAnalysis" type="pythonscript" toolboxalias="stats" gallery_group="Default">
      <displayname>Optimized Hot Spot Analysis</displayname>
      <description>Given incident points or weighted features (points or polygons), creates a map of statistically significant hot and cold spots using the Getis-Ord Gi* statistic. It evaluates the characteristics of the input feature class to produce optimal results.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Statistics" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" gallery_group="Summarize Data">
      <displayname>Summary Statistics</displayname>
      <description>Calculates summary statistics for fields in a table.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="JoinFeatures" type="script" toolboxalias="gapro" attr="spark" gallery_group="Summarize Data">
      <displayname>Join Features</displayname>
      <description>Joins attributes from one layer to another based on spatial, temporal, or attribute relationships, or a combination of those relationships.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="ZonalStatisticsAsTable" type="function" toolboxalias="sa" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Summarize Data">
      <displayname>Zonal Statistics as Table</displayname>
      <description>Summarizes the values of a raster within the zones of another dataset and reports the results as a table.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="PairwiseIntersect" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Overlay Features">
      <displayname>Pairwise Intersect</displayname>
      <description>Computes a pairwise intersection of the input features. Features or portions of features that overlap between the input feature layers or feature classes will be written to the output feature class. Pairwise intersection refers to selecting one feature from the first input and intersecting it with the features in the second input that it overlaps.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Union" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Overlay Features">
      <displayname>Union</displayname>
      <description>Computes a geometric union of the input features. All features and their attributes will be written to the output feature class.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Near" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="edit_session,input_data_change" gallery_group="Determine Proximity">
      <displayname>Near</displayname>
      <description>Calculates distance and additional proximity information between the input features and the closest feature in another layer or feature class.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="GenerateNearTable" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" gallery_group="Determine Proximity">
      <displayname>Generate Near Table</displayname>
      <description>Calculates distances and other proximity information between features in one or more feature classes or layers. Unlike the Near tool, which modifies the input, Generate Near Table writes results to a new stand-alone table and supports finding more than one near feature.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="OptimizedOutlierAnalysis" type="pythonscript" toolboxalias="stats" gallery_group="Analyze Patterns">
      <displayname>Optimized Outlier Analysis</displayname>
      <description>Given incident points or weighted features (points or polygons), creates a map of statistically significant hot spots, cold spots, and spatial outliers using the Anselin Local Moran's I statistic. It evaluates the characteristics of the input feature class to produce optimal results.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="DensityBasedClustering" type="pythonscript" toolboxalias="stats" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Analyze Patterns">
      <displayname>Density-based Clustering</displayname>
      <description>Finds clusters of point features within surrounding noise based on their spatial distribution. Time can also be incorporated to find space-time clusters.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="KernelDensity" type="function" toolboxalias="sa" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Analyze Patterns">
      <displayname>Kernel Density</displayname>
      <description>Calculates a magnitude-per-unit area from point or polyline features using a kernel function to fit a smoothly tapered surface to each point or polyline. A barrier can be used to alter the influence of a feature while calculating kernel density.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="EmpiricalBayesianKriging" type="function" toolboxalias="ga" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Analyze Patterns">
      <displayname>Empirical Bayesian Kriging</displayname>
      <description>Empirical Bayesian kriging is an interpolation method that accounts for the error in estimating the underlying semivariogram through repeated simulations.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Slope" type="function" toolboxalias="sa" attr="GPU" gallery_group="Analyze Terrain">
      <displayname>Slope</displayname>
      <description>Identifies the slope (gradient or steepness) from each cell of a raster.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Contour" type="function" toolboxalias="sa" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Analyze Terrain">
      <displayname>Contour</displayname>
      <description>Creates a feature class of contours from a raster surface.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Viewshed2" type="function" toolboxalias="sa" attr="GPU,mCPU" gallery_group="Analyze Terrain">
      <displayname>Geodesic Viewshed</displayname>
      <description>Determines the raster surface locations visible to a set of observer features using geodesic methods.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Forest" type="script" toolboxalias="stats" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Regression, Prediction, and Classification">
      <displayname>Forest-based and Boosted Classification and Regression</displayname>
      <description>Creates models and generates predictions using one of two supervised machine learning methods: an adaptation of the random forest algorithm developed by Leo Breiman and Adele Cutler or the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) algorithm developed by Tianqi Chen and Carlos Guestrin. Predictions can be performed for both categorical variables (classification) and continuous variables (regression). Explanatory variables can take the form of fields in the attribute table of the training features, raster datasets, and distance features used to calculate proximity values for use as additional variables. In addition to validation of model performance based on the training data, predictions can be made to either features or a prediction raster.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="ClassifyPixelsUsingDeepLearning" type="function" toolboxalias="ia" attr="GPU,mCPU" gallery_group="Regression, Prediction, and Classification">
      <displayname>Classify Pixels Using Deep Learning</displayname>
      <description>Runs a trained deep learning model on an input raster to produce a classified raster, with each valid pixel having an assigned class label.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="DetectObjectsUsingDeepLearning" type="function" toolboxalias="ia" attr="GPU,mCPU" gallery_group="Regression, Prediction, and Classification">
      <displayname>Detect Objects Using Deep Learning</displayname>
      <description>Runs a trained deep learning model on an input raster to produce a feature class containing the objects it finds. The features can be bounding boxes or polygons around the objects found or points at the centers of the objects.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Enrich" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="credits" gallery_group="Enrich Data">
      <displayname>Enrich</displayname>
      <description>Enriches data by adding demographic and landscape facts about the people and places that surround or are inside data locations.  The output is a duplicate of your input with additional attribute fields.  This tool requires an ArcGIS Online organizational account or a locally installed Business Analyst dataset.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="PairwiseDissolve" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Manage Data">
      <displayname>Pairwise Dissolve</displayname>
      <description>Aggregates features based on specified attributes using a parallel processing approach.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Append" type="function" toolboxalias="management" attr="block_add_to_map,edit_session,input_data_change,param_nocount:target" gallery_group="Manage Data">
      <displayname>Append</displayname>
      <description>Appends to, or optionally updates, an existing target dataset with multiple input datasets. Input datasets can be feature classes, tables, shapefiles, rasters, or annotation or dimension feature classes.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Merge" type="function" toolboxalias="management" gallery_group="Manage Data">
      <displayname>Merge</displayname>
      <description>Combines multiple input datasets into a single, new output dataset. This tool can combine point, line, or polygon feature classes or tables.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Project" type="function" toolboxalias="management" gallery_group="Manage Data">
      <displayname>Project</displayname>
      <description>Projects spatial data from one coordinate system to another.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="AddField" type="function" toolboxalias="management" attr="block_add_to_map,input_data_change,param_nocount:in_table" gallery_group="Manage Data">
      <displayname>Add Field</displayname>
      <description>Adds a new field to a table or the table of a feature class or feature layer, as well as to rasters with attribute tables.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="CalculateField" type="function" toolboxalias="management" attr="block_add_to_map,edit_session,input_data_change" gallery_group="Manage Data">
      <displayname>Calculate Field</displayname>
      <description>Calculates the values of a field for a feature class, feature layer, or raster.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="FeatureToPoint" type="function" toolboxalias="management" gallery_group="Convert Data">
      <displayname>Feature To Point</displayname>
      <description>Creates a feature class containing points generated from the centroids of the input features or placed within the input features.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="PointsToLine" type="script" toolboxalias="management" gallery_group="Convert Data">
      <displayname>Points To Line</displayname>
      <description>Creates line features from points.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="FeatureToPolygon" type="function" toolboxalias="management" gallery_group="Convert Data">
      <displayname>Feature To Polygon</displayname>
      <description>Creates a feature class containing polygons generated from areas enclosed by input line or polygon features.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="FeatureToRaster" type="function" toolboxalias="conversion" gallery_group="Convert Data">
      <displayname>Feature to Raster</displayname>
      <description>Converts features to a raster dataset.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="RasterToPoint" type="function" toolboxalias="conversion" gallery_group="Convert Data">
      <displayname>Raster to Point</displayname>
      <description>Converts a raster dataset to point features.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="RasterToPolygon" type="function" toolboxalias="conversion" gallery_group="Convert Data">
      <displayname>Raster to Polygon</displayname>
      <description>Converts a raster dataset to polygon features.</description>
    </tool>
  </gallery>
  <favorites>
    <tool name="CalculateField" type="function" toolboxalias="management" attr="block_add_to_map,edit_session,input_data_change" gallery_group="Project Favorites">
      <displayname>Calculate Field</displayname>
      <description>Calculates the values of a field for a feature class, feature layer, or raster.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="PairwiseBuffer" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Project Favorites">
      <displayname>Pairwise Buffer</displayname>
      <description>Creates buffer polygons around input features to a specified distance using a parallel processing approach.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="Near" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="edit_session,input_data_change" gallery_group="Project Favorites">
      <displayname>Near</displayname>
      <description>Calculates distance and additional proximity information between the input features and the closest feature in another layer or feature class.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="PairwiseDissolve" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Project Favorites">
      <displayname>Pairwise Dissolve</displayname>
      <description>Aggregates features based on specified attributes using a parallel processing approach.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="SpatialJoin" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" gallery_group="Project Favorites">
      <displayname>Spatial Join</displayname>
      <description>Joins attributes from one feature to another based on the spatial relationship. The target features and the joined attributes from the join features are written to the output feature class.</description>
    </tool>
    <tool name="PairwiseIntersect" type="function" toolboxalias="analysis" attr="mCPU" gallery_group="Project Favorites">
      <displayname>Pairwise Intersect</displayname>
      <description>Computes a pairwise intersection of the input features. Features or portions of features that overlap between the input feature layers or feature classes will be written to the output feature class. Pairwise intersection refers to selecting one feature from the first input and intersecting it with the features in the second input that it overlaps.</description>
    </tool>
  </favorites>
</gp_project_root>

<process id="Background#1:21588271190800" ticks="638846925640000000" decimal_point="." err_code="0" has_error="false" version="1" ticks2="638846925844599954">
  <tool name="Tool" type="pythonscript" toolboxalias="toolbox">
    <displayname>Tool</displayname>
